{{- /* editCode shortcode Decorates a fenced code block, turning each $@placeholder$@ inside it into an inline, editable field (a contentEditable span with a pencil icon). Write a normal fenced block inside, with its usual language tag: {{< editCode >}} ```bash curl -fsSL https://example.com/x.yaml | sed 's/old/$@$@/' | kubectl apply -f - ``` {{< /editCode >}} The code is rendered from raw text, not run through Goldmark/Chroma: syntax highlighting wraps tokens in their own spans, which splits the $@...$@ markers and double-escapes the angle brackets inside them, so the replacement can't match. The language tag is kept as a language- class for styling, but tokens aren't coloured. */ -}} {{- $raw := trim .Inner "\n" -}} {{- $lang := "" -}} {{- $code := $raw -}} {{- /* Strip a leading ```lang fence and the trailing ``` fence, if present. */ -}} {{- if hasPrefix $raw "```" -}} {{- $lines := split $raw "\n" -}} {{- $first := index $lines 0 -}} {{- $lang = trim (strings.TrimPrefix "```" $first) " " -}} {{- $body := after 1 $lines -}} {{- $last := sub (len $body) 1 -}} {{- if and (ge $last 0) (hasPrefix (index $body $last) "```") -}} {{- $body = first $last $body -}} {{- end -}} {{- $code = delimit $body "\n" -}} {{- end -}} {{- $codeTag := cond (ne $lang "") (printf "" $lang) "" -}} {{- $escaped := htmlEscape $code -}} {{- $editable := `` -}} {{- $replaced := replaceRE `\$\@([^$]*)\$\@` $editable $escaped -}}
{{ with $lang }}{{ . }}{{ end }} 
{{ $codeTag | safeHTML }}{{ $replaced | safeHTML }}
